longDescription:
Yangon (Rangoon), Myanmar (Burma) The Shwedagon Pagoda (Burmese: Shwedagon Zedi Daw, meaning "Great Dagon Pagoda") is the most sacred
                Buddhist site in Myanmar and one of the most magnificent religious monuments in the world. Rising
                99 meters (325 feet) above Singuttara Hill in Yangon, its gold-plated stupa can be seen from miles
                around, glittering brilliantly in the tropical sun. According to tradition, the pagoda enshrines
                relics of four Buddhas: the staff of Kakusandha, the water filter of Konagamana, a piece of robe
                of Kassapa, and eight hairs of Gautama Buddha given directly to two merchant brothers who met
                him shortly after his enlightenment.
name: Shwedagon Pagoda

description:
Explore temples, mountains, groves, and holy sites that have served as gateways between the human and divine realms
                across cultures and millennia. From ancient stone circles to living pilgrimage destinations, discover the places
                where heaven touches earth.

</svg>
geography: Architectural structure; often positioned at cosmically significant locations; contains sanctum sanctorum and ceremonial chambers

keyMyths.title: The Eight Hairs of the Buddha
keyMyths.description: The Shwedagon Pagoda enshrines eight hairs of Gautama Buddha, obtained by two merchant brothers, Tapussa and Bhallika. These hairs are said to possess extraordinary power and are central to the pagoda's sanctity and the blessings it bestows upon devotees.

keyMyths.title: The Treasure Chamber
keyMyths.description: Legend speaks of a hidden chamber beneath the Shwedagon Pagoda filled with countless treasures donated by kings and devotees over centuries. This chamber is believed to be magically protected and inaccessible to all but the most spiritually advanced.

keyMyths.title: The Wish-Fulfilling Gem
keyMyths.description: At the very top of the Shwedagon Pagoda, embedded within the seinbu (vane), is said to be a wish-fulfilling gem. Devotees believe that sincere prayers offered while gazing at the gem have the power to grant their deepest desires.

keyMyths.title: The Pre-Buddhist Relics
keyMyths.description: Before enshrining the Buddha's hairs, the Shwedagon Pagoda is said to have enshrined relics of three previous Buddhas: Kakusandha's staff, Konagamana's water filter, and Kassapa's robe. This highlights the pagoda's long history and its connection to the lineage of Buddhas.
keyMyths.source: Shwedagon Pagoda History (available at the Pagoda)

keyMyths.title: The Earthquakes and Miraculous Preservation
keyMyths.description: The Shwedagon Pagoda has withstood numerous earthquakes throughout its history. Miraculous stories abound of how the pagoda was preserved despite severe tremors, often attributed to the power of the relics and the merit of devotees.

sources.name: Strachan, Paul. Pagan: Art and Architecture of Old Burma.
sources.description: Provides architectural and historical context for the Shwedagon Pagoda within the broader landscape of Burmese religious structures.
sources.title: Strachan, Paul. Pagan: Art and Architecture of Old Burma.

sources.name: Harvey, G. E. History of Burma: From the Earliest Times to 10 March 1824
sources.description: Offers historical accounts of the Shwedagon Pagoda and its role in Burmese history.
sources.title: Harvey, G. E. History of Burma: From the Earliest Times to 10 March 1824

sources.name: Kalyani Inscriptions
sources.description: A primary source inscription detailing the history of Buddhism in Myanmar, including references to the relics enshrined at Shwedagon.
sources.title: Kalyani Inscriptions

sources.name: Scott, Sir James George. Burma: A Handbook of Practical Information.
sources.description: A historical travel guide providing insights into the Shwedagon Pagoda's cultural significance during the colonial period.
sources.title: Scott, Sir James George. Burma: A Handbook of Practical Information.

sources.name: Aung-Thwin, Michael. The mists of Rāmañña: The Legend that was Lower Burma.
sources.description: Examines the historical and legendary narratives surrounding the foundation of Lower Burma, including stories connected to the Shwedagon Pagoda.
sources.title: Aung-Thwin, Michael. The mists of Rāmañña: The Legend that was Lower Burma.
